Copy, paste, customize.","url":"https://github.com/karanb192/claude-code-hooks"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks: Automate Your AI Coding Workflow","url":"https://www.ksred.com/claude-code-hooks-a-complete-guide-to-automating-your-ai-coding-workflow/"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks: Complete Guide to All 12 Lifecycle Events","url":"https://claudefa.st/blog/tools/hooks/hooks-guide"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks Complete Guide — Automate and Enforce Rules Reliably - SmartScope","url":"https://smartscope.blog/en/generative-ai/claude/claude-code-hooks-guide/"},{"title":"Steering Claude Code: skills, hooks, subagents and more | Claude by Anthropic","url":"https://claude.com/blog/steering-claude-code-skills-hooks-rules-subagents-and-more"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/helderberto/claude-code-hooks-1k7a"}]

Based on the web search results, here's comprehensive information about Claude Code hooks:

## What Are Claude Code Hooks?

Hooks are user-defined shell commands, HTTP endpoints, or LLM prompts that execute automatically at specific points in Claude Code's lifecycle. Think of hooks like git hooks, but for your AI agent—you define handlers that fire automatically at specific points during Claude Code's execution.

## How Hooks Work

Hooks fire at specific points during a Claude Code session. When an event fires and a matcher matches, Claude Code passes JSON context about the event to your hook handler. A hook starts with an event, can optionally be narrowed with a matcher, receives structured input from Claude Code, and then executes one or more handlers.

## Key Advantages Over Prompts

Hooks inject deterministic processing into Claude Code's lifecycle. Unlike prompt instructions, which are requests that Claude may interpret flexibly, hooks execute the same way every time. The critical difference from prompt-based rules is that hooks are guaranteed to execute. If you tell Claude Code in your CLAUDE.md not to modify .env files, it will probably listen. If you set up a PreToolUse hook that blocks writes to .env files, it will always block them.

## Hook Handler Types

Command hooks run a shell command where your script receives the event's JSON input on stdin. HTTP hooks send the event's JSON input as an HTTP POST request to a URL. Prompt hooks send a prompt to a Claude model for single-turn evaluation, with the model returning a yes/no decision as JSON.

## Common Use Cases

Every time Claude Code writes or edits a file, a PostToolUse hook can run your formatter automatically, eliminating reviewing diffs full of formatting inconsistencies. You can set up a PreToolUse hook that inspects every bash command before it runs. The Stop event fires whenever Claude finishes responding, and if your hook returns a blocking signal, Claude is forced to keep working. Combine this with a prompt hook where a separate model evaluates whether all requested tasks were completed and tests were run.
